title: Master File Table (MFT)

headings.text: Why is the MACB Timestamp Important?

overview: - The MFT is the heart of NTFS—a 1 KB file located in the root of every NTFS volume. - Role: Every time Windows creates, modifies, or deletes a file or folder, it writes or updates a corresponding 1 KB record in the MFT. - Forensics Importance: Even after deletion, MFT records can persist (flagged “not in use”), allowing recovery of metadata and, sometimes,

study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
keyConcepts: Why is the MACB Timestamp Important?
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.
